metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Sharpen AC2 with a concrete threshold (e.g. 'cache-hit install time ≤ 12 s, or ≥ 30 % faster than the measured cold baseline of ~19 s') and state the proving command (e.g. compare the 'Run pip install' step duration in the two CI run summaries).
findings.finding: AC2 uses the hedge 'measurably lower' without anchoring to a concrete threshold or proving command, despite the plan body already quantifying 19 s → 9 s. An agent has no objective pass/fail bar for this criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'install time on the cache-hit run is measurably lower than the cold run'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'measurably lower than the cold run' with 'at least 30 % faster than the cold-run install step (baseline ~19 s); evidence: compare the "Run pip install" step durations in the two GitHub Actions run summaries'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the 'What' section's 'every `setup-python` step that installs deps' claim with the AC's explicit scope: either add `external-integration.yml` to the AC, or explicitly call out its exclusion (e.g. 'manually-triggered workflows out of scope') so the scope boundary is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section asserts it covers 'every actions/setup-python step that installs deps', but the AC explicitly scopes to only gerrit-verify.yaml, _optionality.yml, and test.yml. A grep of the workflows directory shows external-integration.yml has two dep-installing setup-python steps (line 35: installs '.[dev]'; line 141: installs a second job's deps) that are not mentioned in the AC. The plan's all-inclusive premise conflicts with its explicit scope.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `.github/workflows/external-integration.yml` to the AC's list, or add a sentence like 'Manually-triggered workflows (`external-integration.yml`, `eval.yml`, etc.) are out of scope for this change' to make the exclusion explicit and intentional.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `eval.yml` (`eval-discipline` job) to the edit-target list and AC, or explicitly justify its exclusion (e.g. 'eval-live already uses actions/cache; eval-discipline intentionally excluded because …').
findings.finding: eval.yml contains a dep-installing setup-python step (eval-discipline job, installs .[dev]) that is not listed as an edit target, violating the plan's own 'all dep-installing setup-python steps' scope statement.
findings.location: .github/workflows/eval.yml — eval-discipline job, lines 44-51; plan section 'What' / AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add `eval.yml` to the AC's file list and apply `cache: 'pip'` + `cache-dependency-path: pyproject.toml` to the setup-python step in the `eval-discipline` job (lines 44-47). For `eval-live`, consider migrating from the manual `actions/cache` block to the `cache: 'pip'` shorthand for consistency, or document the intentional divergence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving step for AC3: push a test commit that adds a dummy version constraint to pyproject.toml (then reverts it), observe 'cache not found' or a new cache key in the Actions log, and record that log line as the evidence.
findings.finding: AC3 requires verifying that a pyproject.toml change invalidates the cache, but the plan describes no proving command or step for this check — an agent has no concrete action to produce evidence of a cache miss.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'A pyproject.toml dependency change invalidates the cache (new key)'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Evidence: in the CI run following the pyproject.toml change, the setup-python step log shows "Cache not found" or a new cache key hash; capture the log line in the PR description.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
